comment.name: Comment
comment.text:
Afternoon Report, 2:45 PM

Good Afternoon Sugarloafers.

If anyone is wearing a hat right now, take it off, because hats off to the groomers. The first early morning run on Hayburner was jaw-dropping. Our groomers are #1 on the hill and #1 in our hearts.

All day, the clouds hung low in the sky. The air gave me a feeling that snow was on its way. Lucky for you all, my instincts (and the weather report) were correct. The snow just started and will continue into the evening. We should wake up with about 3 inches, under partly cloudy skies. Wind will be mild, and temps will be in the 20s at the top and 30s at the base.

Lifts are as follows: West Mountain and Snubber at 8 AM. DRC-B, Sawduster, Skyline, Bucksaw, Skidway, Whiffletree, Superquad, and King Pine at 8:30 AM. Moosecalator at 9 AM.

King Pine will be closing at 3 PM due to the Cross-Haul egress out of the King Pine Bowl being closed.

However, now that it is staying light longer, Superquad will be running until 4:20 PM tomorrow, giving you an extra half hour to play around in the snow.

Groomers will be racing around the hill. They will snag some trails in the West Mountain area, some stuff in the middle, like Gondi and Wedge, and they are even going to try to grab Flume and Widowmaker.

Tomorrow is another day of Spring Demo Days, featuring a new brand every Saturday this spring. Swing by the Sugarshack Demo Center to test the latest in ski technology. Tomorrow is Stöckli Demo Day, where all Stöckli-branded skis are $20 to demo. All other demos are $75. Check out the info in our quick links.

Snowboarders, have you signed up for the Banked Slalom yet? If not, you definitely should. It's gonna be fun. Who knows, you might even win. Registration closes at 5 PM, so make sure you sign up now through our quick links. Swing by the Winterstick Factory tonight for a bib pick-up party and factory tour from 5-7 PM. Enjoy beverages from Orono Brewing Company, and get ready to race.

Stay stoked,
Shannon
